region_of_origin: Vietnam (Central Vietnam, Huế origin); sold in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese tapioca-wrapped shrimp and pork dumplings (Bánh Bột Lọc). Mapped to broader 'dumpling food product' class as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Bánh Bột Lọc. A0FJP (crustacean-based dish) is used as the closest defensible broader category given the primary shrimp filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Vietnamese tapioca shrimp dumplings. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 5 pieces of Bánh Bột Lọc, weighing approximately 120 g, as typically sold at Vietnamese eateries and hawker stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Shrimp Dumplings With Ham (Bánh Bột Lọc) 5Pcs — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for this exact dish entry (cr_id G00010531). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB entry; saturated fat is estimated at 1.2 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable shrimp-and-pork steamed dumplings (USDA SR Legacy #18003 analogy); dietary fibre estimated at 0.5 g/100g from USDA FoodData Central tapioca-based dumpling analogy. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not available in either source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — tapioca starch-based food analogy
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh Bột Lọc in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the GI of tapioca starch-based foods (GI ~65–70 per Atkinson et al., 2021, Am J Clin Nutr) as the primary carbohydrate source. The shrimp and pork protein filling moderates the overall glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 9.64) / 100 ≈ 6.3 g (using 120 g serving × 8.03 g carbohydrate/100g = 9.64 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low intrinsic sodium at 46 mg/100g; primary sodium risk comes from accompanying dipping sauce
hypertension.guidance: Bánh Bột Lọc is suitable for hypertension at the standard 5-piece serving (approximately 55 mg sodium intrinsic); patients should avoid or strictly limit the accompanying fish sauce dipping sauce, which can contribute 200–400 mg additional sodium per tablespoon, and should verify that the ham component is not a high-sodium cured variety.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Tapioca starch wrapper has a medium-to-high GI (~65); moderate carbohydrate load partially offset by protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes may consume the standard 5-piece serving (approximately 9.6 g available carbohydrate, glycaemic load ~6.3) as an occasional dish; the 8.71 g/100g protein content helps moderate postprandial glucose response, but the tapioca starch wrapper should be accounted for in the daily carbohydrate budget, and sweet dipping sauces must be avoided to prevent additional glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.2 g/100g), low sodium (46 mg/100g), and lean shrimp protein; cholesterol at 28 mg/100g is moderate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bánh Bột Lọc presents a low cardiovascular risk profile at the standard serving size due to low saturated fat and sodium content; shrimp contributes dietary cholesterol (approximately 34 mg per serving) which is acceptable within current cardiovascular dietary guidelines for most patients, and the steamed preparation avoids added fats from frying.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork/ham filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6); shrimp provides some omega-3 fatty acids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should prefer the steamed preparation and request shrimp-only filling where possible to reduce arachidonic acid intake from the pork/ham component; sweet dipping sauces with added sugar should be avoided as refined sugar is pro-inflammatory.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Bột Lọc (Shrimp Dumplings with Ham) is a traditional Vietnamese dumpling originating from Huế in Central Vietnam, widely available at Vietnamese eateries and hawker stalls in Singapore. The dumpling wrapper is made from tapioca starch (bột lọc), which becomes translucent and slightly chewy when steamed or briefly boiled, giving the dumpling a distinctive crystal-clear appearance. The filling consists of whole or chopped shrimp combined with seasoned pork or ham, flavoured with fish sauce, black pepper, garlic, and shallots. A standard serving is 5 pieces (approximately 120 g) and is typically served with a sweet-sour fish sauce dipping sauce (nước chấm). Nutritionally, the dish provides 101 kcal per 100 g, with a notable protein content of 8.71 g/100g from the shrimp and pork filling, moderate fat at 3.78 g/100g, and a relatively low carbohydrate content of 8.03 g/100g from the tapioca wrapper. Sodium is very low at 46 mg/100g intrinsically, though the dipping sauce can significantly increase sodium intake. The tapioca starch wrapper has a medium glycaemic index (~65), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free, making it suitable for those with gluten intolerance. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the shrimp and pork content. Clinically, it is a low-sodium, moderate-protein dish appropriate for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management when consumed without high-sodium dipping sauces.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010531, confidence 0.95 in source record), which is the primary approved source for Singapore-sold dishes. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous data and clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework for tapioca-based foods, which is the approved GI source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable steamed shrimp-and-pork dumplings; not directly reported in the HPB source entry. 2. Dietary fibre (0.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central tapioca-based dumpling analogy; not directly reported in the HPB source entry. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese tapioca dumpling preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. Halal status is marked false as the dish contains pork/ham; individual vendors may offer halal-certified variants. 5. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on tapioca starch GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database framework (Atkinson et al., 2021); no direct GI measurement for Bánh Bột Lọc exists in the approved GI database. 6. The rheumatoid arthritis health context from the input record has been preserved and migrated to additional_conditions. 7. The input health_suitability entry for rheumatoid_arthritis had an internally inconsistent reason field (stated suitable:false but reason described anti-inflammatory properties); the output health_context has been corrected to reflect moderate concern with accurate clinical guidance.
